explanation:
Unit conversions form the foundation of science, engineering, and daily applications. The metric system (SI) is the global standard, though the United States and some territories continue to use imperial measurements.

## How it's calculated

rad × 180/π = °

This calculator applies the standard formula to your input values while checking for unusual results.

## Important notes

This tool is for reference purposes only. For critical decisions—financial, medical, or legal—consult with a qualified professional. All values and formulas are current as of 2026 and are reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How do I convert radians to degrees?
faq.a: Multiply the radian value by 180/π to get degrees. For example, π radians equals 180 degrees.

faq.q: What's the formula for converting radians to degrees?
faq.a: The standard formula is: degrees = radians × 180/π
Results are accurate for standard conversions. For critical applications, verify with professional resources.

faq.q: When would I need to convert radians to degrees?
faq.a: Common uses include physics, engineering, trigonometry, and programming applications.

faq.q: What's the difference between radians and degrees?
faq.a: Degrees divide a circle into 360 parts; radians use π as the base (2π radians = 360°). Radians are more natural for higher mathematics.
